content:
# Numbers Chapter 27 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 181-182 | **Source Image:** `NUM_027_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_027_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Manuscript:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0134-pa_0135

---

**1.** And the daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, of the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph, drew near -- and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah --

**2.** and they stood before Moses and before Eleazar the priest and before the leaders and all the congregation, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, saying:

**3.** "Our father died in the wilderness, and he was not among the congregation of those who gathered themselves together against YHWH in the congregation of Korah, but in his own sin he died, and he had no sons.

**4.** Why should the name of our father be withdrawn from the midst of his clan because he had no son? Give to us a possession among the brothers of our father."

**5.** And Moses brought their case before YHWH.

**6.** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**7.** "Rightly the daughters of Zelophehad speak. You shall surely give to them a possession of an inheritance among the brothers of their father, and you shall cause the inheritance of their father to pass over to them.

**8.** And to the sons of Israel you shall speak, saying: 'If a man dies and has no son, then you shall cause his inheritance to pass over to his daughter.

**9.** And if he has no daughter, then you shall give his inheritance to his brothers.

**10.** And if he has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to the brothers of his father.

**11.** And if his father has no brothers, then you shall give his inheritance to his kinsman who is nearest to him from his clan, and he shall possess it.' And it shall be for the sons of Israel a statute of judgment, as YHWH commanded Moses."

**12.** And YHWH said to Moses: "Go up to this mountain of Abarim and see the land which I have given to the sons of Israel.

**13.** And when you have seen it, you also shall be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother was gathered,

**14.** because you rebelled against my word in the wilderness of Zin, in the strife of the congregation, to sanctify me at the waters before their eyes." These are the waters of Meribah of Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin.

**15.** And Moses spoke to YHWH, saying:

**16.** "Let YHWH, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation,

**17.** who shall go out before them and who shall come in before them, and who shall lead them out and who shall bring them in, so that the congregation of YHWH shall not be as sheep which have no shepherd."

**18.** And YHWH said to Moses: "Take for yourself Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom is spirit, and lay your hand upon him.

**19.** And set him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation, and commission him before their eyes.

**20.** And you shall put some of your splendor upon him, so that all the congregation of the sons of Israel may obey.

**21.** And before Eleazar the priest he shall stand, and he shall inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim before YHWH. At his word they shall go out and at his word they shall come in, he and all the sons of Israel with him, and all the congregation."

**22.** And Moses did as YHWH commanded him. And he took Joshua and set him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation.

**23.** And he laid his hands upon him and commissioned him, as YHWH spoke by the hand of Moses.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**Verse 6:** All three translators rendered this verse identically in substance. EMET uses a colon after "saying," LOGOS and PNEUMA use a comma; SOPHIA follows EMET's colon as consistent with project convention for direct speech introduction.

**Verse 9:** Complete agreement across all three translators. Adopted as-is.

**Verse 10:** Complete agreement across all three translators. Adopted as-is.

**Verse 15:** Complete agreement across all three translators. Adopted as-is.

**Verse 23:** All three translators agree on the substance: hands (plural), commissioned, "by the hand of Moses." PNEUMA's "just as YHWH had spoken through Moses" paraphrases the idiom, but EMET and LOGOS preserve the literal "by the hand of Moses." SOPHIA follows the literal rendering. Minor divergence from PNEUMA noted in the Disagreement Log.

---

## Disagreement Log

### Verse 1
- **EMET:** "drew near -- and these [are] the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Tirzah" / "of the families of Manasseh"
- **LOGOS:** "drew near -- and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah" / "of the clans of Manasseh"
- **PNEUMA:** "stepped forward" (restructured sentence, placing genealogy before the approach); "from the clans of Manasseh"; "These were the names"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "drew near" (EMET/LOGOS), which is the standard rendering of Hebrew ותקרבנה, confirmed in both MT and LXX (προσῆλθον). "Clans" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is preferred over "families" (EMET) for מִשְׁפְּחֹת, as "clan" better reflects the social unit between tribe and household. The parenthetical structure of EMET/LOGOS is preserved rather than PNEUMA's restructuring. "And" conjunctions between daughter names (EMET) are simplified to follow LOGOS/PNEUMA comma-list format, which is cleaner in English while preserving all five names in their Masoretic order.

### Verse 2
- **EMET:** "all the congregation, [at] the opening of the tent of meeting, saying:"
- **LOGOS:** "all the congregation, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, saying,"
- **PNEUMA:** "the entire community, at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, and said:"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "all the congregation" (EMET/LOGOS) per project convention for עֵדָה. "Entrance" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is preferred over "opening" (EMET) for פֶּתַח, as both are valid but "entrance" is more standard. "Tent of meeting" is lowercase (EMET/LOGOS) per convention. "Saying" is retained over PNEUMA's "and said" to preserve the Hebrew participial construction לֵאמֹר.

### Verse 3
- **EMET:** "he was not in the midst of the congregation, the ones who gathered themselves together against YHWH in the congregation of Korah, but in his [own] sin he died, and sons he did not have"
- **LOGOS:** "he was not among the congregation of those who gathered together against YHWH in the congregation of Korah, but he died in his own sin, and he had no sons"
- **PNEUMA:** "He was not among the faction that banded together against YHWH -- the faction of Korah -- but he died for his own sin, and he had no sons"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "among the congregation of those who gathered themselves together" (blending EMET's reflexive "gathered themselves" with LOGOS's cleaner structure). "Congregation" is retained over PNEUMA's "faction" for עֵדָה, per project convention. "In his own sin he died" preserves the Hebrew word order (בְּחֶטְאוֹ מֵת) more closely than LOGOS's inverted form, while adding "own" for clarity as all three translators agree on. "He had no sons" follows LOGOS/PNEUMA over EMET's more wooden "sons he did not have."

### Verse 4
- **EMET:** "be withdrawn from the midst of his family" / "Give to us a possession in the midst of the brothers of our father"
- **LOGOS:** "be withdrawn from the midst of his clan" / "Give to us a possession among the brothers of our father"
- **PNEUMA:** "disappear from his clan" / "Give us a land holding among our father's kinsmen"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "withdrawn" (EMET/LOGOS) for יִגָּרַע rather than PNEUMA's "disappear," preserving the niphal passive nuance. "Clan" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) for מִשְׁפָּחָה is preferred over "family" (EMET) for consistency with verse 1. "Among the brothers of our father" (LOGOS) is adopted over "in the midst of" (EMET, more wooden) and "among our father's kinsmen" (PNEUMA, which replaces "brothers" with the more general "kinsmen," not matching Hebrew אֲחֵי). "Possession" is retained (EMET/LOGOS) over "land holding" (PNEUMA) for אֲחֻזָּה, as the legal term is broader.

### Verse 5
- **EMET:** "their judgment"
- **LOGOS:** "their case"
- **PNEUMA:** "their case"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "their case" (LOGOS/PNEUMA majority) for מִשְׁפָּטָן. While "judgment" is a valid rendering, "case" better fits the context of bringing a legal matter before YHWH for adjudication, since the judgment has not yet been rendered.

### Verse 7
- **EMET:** "Rightly the daughters of Zelophehad speak"
- **LOGOS:** "The daughters of Zelophehad speak rightly"
- **PNEUMA:** "The daughters of Zelophehad are right in what they say" / "You must certainly give them a hereditary land holding among their father's kinsmen, and transfer their father's inheritance to them"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts EMET's word order "Rightly the daughters of Zelophehad speak," which preserves the emphatic fronting of כֵּן in the Hebrew. LOGOS places "rightly" at the end, losing this emphasis. PNEUMA paraphrases entirely. For the inheritance clause, "You shall surely give" (EMET/LOGOS) preserves the infinitive absolute נָתֹן תִּתֵּן. "Possession of an inheritance" (EMET/LOGOS) is preferred over "hereditary land holding" (PNEUMA). "Among the brothers" is retained over PNEUMA's "kinsmen." "You shall cause the inheritance of their father to pass over to them" (EMET/LOGOS) is preferred over PNEUMA's "transfer."

### Verse 8
- **EMET:** "If a man dies and he has no son"
- **LOGOS:** "If a man dies and has no son"
- **PNEUMA:** "When a man dies without leaving a son" / "speak to the Sons of Israel"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS's streamlined "If a man dies and has no son." PNEUMA's "When" changes the conditional אִם to a temporal clause, and "without leaving" is interpretive. "Sons of Israel" capitalization: SOPHIA uses lowercase "sons" per project convention (EMET/LOGOS), not PNEUMA's capitalized "Sons."

### Verse 11
- **EMET:** "if there are no brothers to his father" / "his kinsman, the one nearest to him from his family"
- **LOGOS:** "if his father has no brothers" / "his kinsman who is nearest to him from his clan"
- **PNEUMA:** "if his father has no brothers" / "his nearest relative within his clan" / "a binding rule of law"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS's phrasing "if his father has no brothers" (cleaner English, LOGOS/PNEUMA majority). "Kinsman who is nearest to him from his clan" (LOGOS) balances literalness with clarity. PNEUMA's "nearest relative" loses שְׁאֵרוֹ (kinsman). "Statute of judgment" (EMET/LOGOS) is retained over PNEUMA's "binding rule of law" for לְחֻקַּת מִשְׁפָּט, preserving the Hebrew legal terminology.

### Verse 12
- **EMET:** "see the land which I have given"
- **LOGOS:** "see the land that I have given"
- **PNEUMA:** "look out over the land that I have given" / "mountain of the Abarim range"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "see the land which I have given" (EMET's "which" is slightly more formal and fitting for divine speech). PNEUMA's "look out over" adds interpretive expansion not present in the Hebrew וּרְאֵה ("and see"). "Mountain of Abarim" (EMET/LOGOS) is retained over PNEUMA's "mountain of the Abarim range," as "range" is an addition not in the Hebrew הַר הָעֲבָרִים.

### Verse 13
- **EMET:** "And you shall see it, and you shall be gathered to your people, also you, as Aaron your brother was gathered"
- **LOGOS:** "And when you have seen it, you also shall be gathered to your people, as Aaron your brother was gathered"
- **PNEUMA:** "And when you have seen it, you too will be gathered to your people, just as your brother Aaron was gathered"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts LOGOS's rendering, which balances literalness with readability. "When you have seen it" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is a temporal clause that captures the Hebrew sequence better than EMET's coordinate clauses. "You also" (LOGOS) preserves גַּם אַתָּה. "As Aaron your brother" (EMET/LOGOS) preserves Hebrew word order over PNEUMA's "your brother Aaron." PNEUMA's "just as" and "will be" (future rather than shall) are minor dynamic adjustments not adopted.

### Verse 14
- **EMET:** "because you rebelled against my word" / "These [are] the waters of Meribah of Kadesh, [in] the wilderness of Zin"
- **LOGOS:** "because you rebelled against my word" / "These are the waters of Meribah of Kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin"
- **PNEUMA:** "because you both rebelled against my command" / "you failed to uphold my holiness" / "(These are the waters of Meribah-kadesh, in the wilderness of Zin.)"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS: "my word" for פִּי (literally "my mouth"), and "to sanctify me" for לְהַקְדִּישֵׁנִי. PNEUMA adds "both" (not explicit in the Hebrew at this point) and paraphrases "to sanctify me" as "to uphold my holiness," which is interpretive. PNEUMA's "Meribah-kadesh" (hyphenated compound) differs from the construct chain "Meribah of Kadesh" (EMET/LOGOS), which better reflects מְרִיבַת קָדֵשׁ. SOPHIA omits EMET's brackets, following LOGOS's clean rendering.

### Verse 16
- **EMET:** "the God of the spirits of all flesh"
- **LOGOS:** "the God of the spirits of all flesh"
- **PNEUMA:** "the God of the spirits of all living beings" / "appoint someone over the community"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA retains "all flesh" (EMET/LOGOS) for כָל־בָּשָׂר, which is the standard biblical idiom. PNEUMA's "all living beings" is interpretive. "A man" (EMET/LOGOS) is retained over PNEUMA's "someone" for אִישׁ. "Congregation" (EMET/LOGOS) over "community" (PNEUMA) per project convention.

### Verse 17
- **EMET:** "and the congregation of YHWH shall not be as sheep which have no shepherd"
- **LOGOS:** "so that the congregation of YHWH may not be as sheep that have no shepherd"
- **PNEUMA:** "so that the community of YHWH will not be like sheep without a shepherd"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "so that" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) as a purpose clause rendering of וְלֹא, which captures the intent better than EMET's simple conjunction. "Shall not be" (EMET) is adjusted to "shall not be" (retaining the stronger modal over LOGOS's "may not be" and PNEUMA's "will not be"). "Sheep which have no shepherd" (EMET) is preferred over PNEUMA's simplified "sheep without a shepherd" for preserving the relative clause אֲשֶׁר אֵין־לָהֶם רֹעֶה.

### Verse 18
- **EMET:** "Take to yourself Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom [is] spirit, and you shall lay your hand upon him"
- **LOGOS:** "Take for yourself Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom is the spirit, and lay your hand upon him"
- **PNEUMA:** "Take Joshua son of Nun, a man in whom the spirit dwells, and lay your hand upon him"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "Take for yourself" (LOGOS) for לְךָ, splitting the difference between EMET's "to yourself" and PNEUMA's omission of the reflexive. "A man in whom is spirit" -- SOPHIA omits the article "the" before "spirit" (following EMET), since the Hebrew רוּחַ is anarthrous here, and the ambiguity is theologically significant. PNEUMA's "dwells" adds interpretation not in the Hebrew. "And lay your hand upon him" (LOGOS/PNEUMA imperative) is adopted over EMET's "and you shall lay" for smoother English following the initial imperative קַח.

### Verse 19
- **EMET:** "And you shall stand him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation, and you shall commission him before their eyes"
- **LOGOS:** "And set him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation, and commission him before their eyes"
- **PNEUMA:** "And stand him before Eleazar the priest and before the entire community, and commission him in their sight"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "set him" (LOGOS) for וְהַעֲמַדְתָּ, which is natural English for the hiphil of עמד. "Before their eyes" (EMET/LOGOS) is retained over PNEUMA's "in their sight" for לְעֵינֵיהֶם, preserving the concrete Hebrew idiom. "All the congregation" over "entire community" per convention.

### Verse 20
- **EMET:** "And you shall put from your splendor upon him, so that all the congregation of the sons of Israel may hear [him]"
- **LOGOS:** "And you shall put some of your splendor upon him, so that all the congregation of the sons of Israel may obey"
- **PNEUMA:** "And invest him with some of your authority, so that the entire community of the Sons of Israel will obey him"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "some of your splendor" (LOGOS) -- "some of" renders the partitive מִן more naturally than EMET's "from." "Splendor" (EMET/LOGOS) for הוֹד is retained over PNEUMA's "authority," which is interpretive. "May obey" (LOGOS) for יִשְׁמְעוּ is adopted: the Hebrew שׁמע can mean both "hear" and "obey," but in the context of authority transfer, "obey" is more fitting. EMET's "hear" is more literal but misses the contextual force. PNEUMA's "invest him with" restructures the Hebrew too freely.

### Verse 21
- **EMET:** "And before Eleazar the priest he shall stand, and he shall inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim before YHWH"
- **LOGOS:** "And before Eleazar the priest he shall stand, and he shall inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim before YHWH"
- **PNEUMA:** "And he shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who will seek guidance for him through the judgment of the Urim before YHWH"
- **Resolution:** EMET and LOGOS agree exactly and preserve the Hebrew fronted word order ("before Eleazar the priest he shall stand"). PNEUMA restructures to standard English order and paraphrases "inquire" as "seek guidance." SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS.

### Verse 22
- **EMET:** "And he took Joshua and stood him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation"
- **LOGOS:** "And he took Joshua and set him before Eleazar the priest and before all the congregation"
- **PNEUMA:** "He took Joshua and stood him before Eleazar the priest and before the entire community"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "set him" (LOGOS) for consistency with verse 19 where the same root (עמד hiphil) was rendered "set." PNEUMA drops the initial "And," which SOPHIA retains per convention of preserving Hebrew conjunctions.

### Verse 23
- **EMET:** "as YHWH spoke by the hand of Moses"
- **LOGOS:** "as YHWH spoke by the hand of Moses"
- **PNEUMA:** "just as YHWH had spoken through Moses"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS: "as YHWH spoke by the hand of Moses," preserving the Hebrew idiom בְּיַד מֹשֶׁה literally. PNEUMA's "through Moses" paraphrases the idiom, and "had spoken" (pluperfect) adds a tense nuance not required by the Hebrew.

---
*Produced by SOPHIA in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
